jokes.text: Joe, that's why your parents told you not to jump on the bed!
jokes.setup: Adult Joey apparently injured himself jumping on his bed
jokes.punchline: Being scolded like a child for childish behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple character-based humor about Joey's childlike behavior, economically delivered

jokes.text: Hey, look at me! I'm making jam! I've been at it since 4 a.m.
jokes.punchline: The obsessive 4 AM start time reveals her manic state
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Monica's obsessive tendencies through extreme behavior

jokes.text: Down at the docks. You know, you can get it wholesale. / I didn't know there were docks.
jokes.setup: Monica casually mentions getting fruit at the docks
jokes.punchline: The innocent revelation that someone doesn't know NYC has docks
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with efficient setup/payoff about NYC geography ignorance

jokes.text: You tell the doctor you did it jumping on your bed? / No. I had a whole story worked out, but then... Chandler sold me out.
jokes.setup: Joey apparently has an elaborate lie prepared
jokes.punchline: Chandler betrayed him by telling the truth
jokes.explanation: Character-driven humor about Joey's elaborate lies and Chandler's practical nature

jokes.text: I didn't think the doctor would buy that it 'fell out of the socket'.
jokes.setup: Joey's ridiculous explanation for his injury
jokes.punchline: Chandler's dry dismissal of the obviously impossible story
jokes.explanation: Strong Chandler joke with perfect deadpan delivery and absurd premise

jokes.text: Jam? I love jam! How come we never have jam at our place? / Because the kids need new shoes.
jokes.setup: Joey wants jam at their apartment
jokes.punchline: Chandler's sarcastic parental responsibility excuse
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic humor playing with domestic/parental clichés

jokes.text: I'm sick of being depressed about Richard. I needed a plan. A plan to get over my man. What's the opposite of man? Jam!
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous man/jam wordplay as problem-solving
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good character integration and memorable delivery structure

jokes.text: Joey, don't! It's way too hot!
jokes.punchline: Joey presumably burns himself on the jam
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy setup, predictable but fits Joey's character

jokes.text: This'll just be my batch.
jokes.punchline: His resigned acceptance and claiming of the contaminated batch
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Joey's practical acceptance of consequences

jokes.text: One more chance, Ursula. Please. / This is awkward. Yeah, because you want Ursula, and I'm Phoebe. Twin sisters.
jokes.setup: Guy desperately pleading to wrong twin
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's matter-of-fact correction of the awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Standard mistaken identity setup with good cringe factor

jokes.text: I am such a dingus! / Oh, you're not a dingus.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sweet reassurance about the silly word
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the silly word 'dingus' and Phoebe's kindness

jokes.text: Once, in junior high school, I thought I was a witch. And a guidance counselor told me something that might help you. He said, 'Okay, you're not a witch. You're just an average student.' / See what I'm saying? / Not really.
jokes.setup: Phoebe offers helpful advice through personal story
jokes.punchline: The completely unhelpful and nonsensical guidance counselor advice
jokes.explanation: Excellent Phoebe absurdist humor with perfect misdirection and character voice

jokes.text: Well, get over it.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's blunt, unhelpful conclusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character voice, great deadpan delivery

jokes.text: You don't have to walk behind me anymore.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's casual permission to walk normally
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phoebe's unconventional approach to the stalking situation

jokes.text: It's an empty apartment. We're all alone in an empty apartment. / I have to be at work in 10 minutes. / All right. It's not like I'm employee of the year or anything.
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-deprecating work excuse
jokes.explanation: Solid character humor about Chandler's work attitude and romantic priorities

jokes.text: Do I look fat? / No. / I accept that.
jokes.setup: The loaded 'do I look fat' question
jokes.punchline: Chandler's formal acceptance of the safe answer
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor with efficient delivery and Chandler's formal tone

jokes.text: When Janice asked me and I said no, she thought I was calling her a cow.
jokes.punchline: The misunderstanding escalation to 'cow'
jokes.explanation: Standard relationship humor with good escalation from fat to cow

jokes.text: You looked at her? / You never look. You just answer. It's like a reflex. 'Do I look fat?' 'No.' 'Is she prettier than I am?' 'No.'
jokes.punchline: The robotic relationship reflex responses
jokes.explanation: Strong observational humor about relationship dynamics with good rhythm

jokes.text: 'Does size matter?' / And it works both ways.
jokes.punchline: The mutual deception acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the relationship lies theme with economy

jokes.text: Janice is coming back from a trip. She gives you two options. Option one: She'll take a cab home from the airport. Option two: You can meet her at baggage claim. What do you do? / Easy. Baggage claim. / Wrong! Now you're single.
jokes.setup: Seemingly obvious choice between airport options
jokes.punchline: The wrong answer trap and dramatic consequence
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with strong setup/payoff structure and dramatic stakes

jokes.text: It's actually secret option number three. You meet her at the gate. That way she knows you love her.
jokes.punchline: The hidden correct answer with romantic logic
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the setup with relatable relationship wisdom

jokes.text: Sorry, honey. We can't help you there, because we're cuddly sleepers.
jokes.punchline: Ross claiming he can't help due to being cuddly
jokes.explanation: Mild humor setting up the cuddling contradiction that follows

jokes.text: I thought you were 'cuddly sleepers'. / No, not cuddly. Not me. Just her. I'm like you. I need the room.
jokes.setup: Ross's previous claim about being cuddly
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction and admission of deception
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's willingness to lie for male solidarity

jokes.text: Now, you wait for her to drift off... and then you hug her... and roll her over to her side of the bed. And then you... roll away.
jokes.punchline: The elaborate physical manipulation technique
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with relatable relationship problem-solving

jokes.text: Hug for her. Roll for you.
jokes.punchline: The simplified, selfish summary
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure, very quotable summary of the technique

jokes.text: The old 'hug and roll'. One question. You're pretending the pillow's a girl, right?
jokes.punchline: Chandler's practical clarification about the demo
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler character beat with practical observation about the demonstration

jokes.text: Remember when you were a kid, your mom would send you to the movies... with a jar of jam and a little spoon?
jokes.punchline: The bizarre jam-and-spoon movie snack memory
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific absurdist humor showing Monica's food-centric worldview

jokes.text: You're so pretty.
jokes.punchline: Her delusional affection for the jam
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Monica's unhealthy relationship with her jam project

jokes.text: First, I'm not crazy. And second, say it, don't spray it.
jokes.punchline: Her practical concern about spitting while talking
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback with good Phoebe character voice and practical concern

jokes.text: Because of the restraining order.
jokes.setup: Explanation of why Malcolm couldn't approach directly
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of restraining order
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with efficient delivery and casual treatment of serious topic

jokes.text: Oh, my God! You've got a crush on your sister's stalker.
jokes.punchline: Monica's blunt assessment of the situation
jokes.explanation: Strong observational humor with good economy and quotable phrasing

jokes.text: I didn't stalk her. / I asked for the news, not the weather.
jokes.punchline: Someone's dismissive weather/news quip
jokes.explanation: Mild humor with standard comeback structure, somewhat forced

jokes.text: Joe, I got to ask. The girl from the xerox place, buck naked... or a big tub of jam? / Put your hands together.
jokes.setup: Chandler's sexual fantasy vs jam question
jokes.punchline: Joey's gesture indicating he wants both
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Joey's hedonistic nature and food priorities

jokes.text: No more jam? What happened to your jam plan? / I figured out I'd need to charge $17 a jar just to break even. So I've got a new plan now. Babies.
jokes.punchline: The sudden leap to babies as the new plan
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character work, great comedic timing and absurd logic

jokes.text: Well, you'll need much bigger jars.
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation continuing jam logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Joey logic and perfect economy, great callback structure and timing

jokes.text: Aren't you forgetting something? What is that guy's name? Dad!
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's dramatic 'Dad' revelation
jokes.explanation: Good structure and delivery showing Phoebe's dramatic style

jokes.text: I don't need an actual man. Just a few of his best swimmers. / Down to the docks again?
jokes.punchline: Chandler's callback to dock fruit shopping
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback structure connecting sperm shopping to fruit shopping at docks

jokes.text: Look at all the room on her side! A giant penguin could fit over there. That'd be weird, though.
jokes.setup: Chandler's internal monologue about bed space
jokes.punchline: The random penguin imagery and second-thought
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Chandler humor with unexpected penguin imagery

jokes.text: Hug and roll time. I'm hugging. I'm hugging. You're rolling, and... Yes! Freedom! Except for this arm! I'm stuck. Stuck arm.
jokes.setup: Chandler attempting the hug and roll
jokes.punchline: The technique failing with his arm trapped
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy payoff to earlier setup with realistic failure

jokes.text: Time for the old tablecloth trick. One fluid motion. Quick like a cat. And one, two... three!
jokes.punchline: Attempting magic trick solution with dramatic buildup
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the physical comedy with theatrical elements

jokes.text: Here's my binoculars. / These are my night-vision goggles. This is the book I pretend to read when I'm watching her in the park.
jokes.punchline: The increasingly elaborate and creepy surveillance tools
jokes.explanation: Good escalating cringe humor revealing the extent of stalking behavior

jokes.text: And these are 'Madlibs'. They're just for fun.
jokes.punchline: The innocent Madlibs among the creepy tools
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of innocent fun with stalking tools, great economy

jokes.text: 'I met Phoebe today.' 'She was really nice to me even though I'm such a loser.' 'And when I was walking home, I thought about her a lot.' 'It was weird, but kind of cool.'
jokes.punchline: The sweet, innocent entries about Phoebe instead of Ursula
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Malcolm's genuine feelings shifting to Phoebe

jokes.text: So what were you thinking? / I was thinking what it'd be like to kiss you. / Really? / No. That's just something I said now so that maybe I could kiss you.
jokes.punchline: Malcolm's honest admission of his manipulative line
jokes.explanation: Excellent honest/manipulative humor with good structure and character work

jokes.text: No, it's all right. I just had a jar of mustard.
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's random mustard consumption confession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe absurdist humor with excellent character integration and originality

jokes.text: He describes himself as a male Gina Davis. / You mean there's more than one of us?
jokes.punchline: Character thinking they're like Geena Davis
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity reference humor with solid setup/payoff structure

jokes.text: You can't do this, Mon. If you do this, I'll... / You'll what? / I'll tell Mom.
jokes.punchline: The childish 'tell Mom' threat
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic humor with Ross reverting to childhood tactics

jokes.text: It may not be ideal, but... / Oh, it's not... / Lips moving. Still talking.
jokes.punchline: Monica's sarcastic observation about his continued talking
jokes.explanation: Solid sibling banter with Monica's characteristic directness

jokes.text: Oh, my God! Under personal comments: 'New York Knicks rule.' / Yeah, the Knicks rule! / Joey, this is you!
jokes.punchline: Joey enthusiastically agreeing before realizing it's his profile
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal structure with perfect Joey character moment and recognition

jokes.text: You went to a sperm bank? / Right after I did that sex study at NYU. Remember that sweater I gave you? / That's how you bought it? / No. That's what I was wearing when I donated.
jokes.punchline: The sweater was his donation outfit, not purchased with money
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and misdirection with perfect Joey logic and grossness

jokes.text: I'm surprised there's any of my boys left.
jokes.punchline: Joey's cocky assumption about demand for his sperm
jokes.explanation: Good Joey confidence humor with characteristic self-importance

jokes.text: You're taking the word of a guy who has night-vision goggles?
jokes.punchline: Monica pointing out the obvious red flag of night-vision goggles
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor with excellent economy and logical point

jokes.text: Wake up and smell the restraining order.
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'wake up and smell the coffee' with restraining order
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy and memorable phrase structure

jokes.text: Crazy Chandler spun me... off the bed. / Wow! Spinning! Sounds like fun. / I wish. He was just trying Ross' 'hug and roll' thing.
jokes.setup: Janice describing being spun off the bed
jokes.punchline: The hug and roll technique gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup showing the technique's spectacular failure

jokes.text: Oh... my... God!
jokes.setup: Learning about the hug and roll technique
jokes.punchline: Janice's dramatic realization of the deception
jokes.explanation: Classic Janice reaction with perfect character voice and dramatic delivery

jokes.text: Well, what do you know? There goes my identical twin sister! Just walking along, looking like me.
jokes.punchline: Her sarcastic 'coincidence' explanation about twin sister
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic humor with Phoebe's characteristic style when caught

jokes.text: Is this a freakish coincidence, or did you know she takes this train?
jokes.punchline: Malcolm calling out Phoebe's obvious stalking
jokes.explanation: Good logical response to Phoebe's weak excuse with direct confrontation

jokes.text: Stalk me for a while, huh? I'll be like an Ursula patch.
jokes.setup: Phoebe helping Malcolm get over Ursula
jokes.punchline: The nicotine patch analogy for stalking addiction
jokes.explanation: Excellent original concept with perfect analogy and character voice, highly quotable

jokes.text: Sperm or regular?
jokes.punchline: Joey's casual distinction between bank types
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and perfect Joey practicality with callback integration

jokes.text: I always pictured you with a tall, smart, blond guy with a name like... Hoyt. / It's a name. Yeah.
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'Hoyt' and Monica's polite acceptance
jokes.explanation: Good absurd name humor with sibling dynamic and Monica's diplomatic response

jokes.text: You have a sign that says: 'We don't swim in your toilet. Don't pee in our pool.' / We don't have that sign. / You do! It was a gift from me.
jokes.setup: Ross describing Monica's future house details
jokes.punchline: The tacky pool sign he gave as a gift
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing Ross's gift-giving taste and sibling dynamics

jokes.text: Wow! This guy's an astronaut? / That would have been cool... for a day.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward space travel career
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing casual dismissal of impressive achievement

jokes.text: I called the sperm bank. They haven't sold a single unit of Tribbiani. Nobody wants my product.
jokes.setup: Joey's confidence about sperm bank demand
jokes.punchline: The harsh reality of zero sales
jokes.explanation: Good comeuppance humor with Joey's ego deflation and business terminology

jokes.text: Maybe if they met me in person.
jokes.punchline: His delusional solution involving personal meetings
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic showing his misunderstanding of the sperm donation process

jokes.text: You got a thing on your... Get it? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Joey pointing out something on Chandler
jokes.punchline: The physical removal and confirmation
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy moment, minimal craft but fits the scene flow

jokes.text: Chan, can I talk to you for a second? / Just one additional relationship thought. Something you're probably aware of. Women talk.
jokes.punchline: The ominous revelation about women sharing information
jokes.explanation: Good setup for consequences with ominous delivery and universal truth
